lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Nilsal to you, iknami. Welcome to the Grand Museum. I hope your visit is a pleasant one. If you have any questions about Varlamore, I'm happy to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the early history of Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Certainly! When it comes to the existence of Varlamore, it is Xeric we have to thank. During his Age of Strife, many refugees fled Kourend over the previously unexplored Custodia Mountains.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: There were three notable groups of these refugees. The first group, the Talkasti, settled in the Tlati Rainforest.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: While they initially lived nomadic lives, they eventually started to establish more permanent settlements, with Tal Teklan being the first.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: The next group were the Pericans. They first settled right here, building the town of Fortis, or Civitas illa Fortis as it eventually became.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Finally we have the dwarves. These were refugees from Lovakengj who established a home beneath Ralos' Rise, becoming known as the Dwarves of Cam Torum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was there any friction between these groups initially?

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Oh yes, very much so. The dwarves, just as they do today, mostly kept to themselves. However, the Talkasti and Pericans came into regular conflict over various matters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But that's not the case now, right? What brought them together?

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: As you may well have already guessed, it was religion. Long before leaving Kourend, the Talkasti had taken to secretly worshipping Ralos and Ranul.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: After arriving here, they were able to openly practice their religion for the first time. Over time, the Pericans started to adopt this religion as well.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: As the two groups came together, the population of the region started to rapidly expand. This eventually led to the construction of Tempestus, and with it, the founding of Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So are there still distinctions between the Talkasti and the Pericans now?

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Yes and no. Those terms are no longer used to describe people today. Since we united, we have all been Varlamorians. However, you'll still find many traces of our previous divide.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: For example, here in Fortis, you'll find yourself surrounded by Perican archetecture, whereas over in the Tlati Rainforest, the influence of the Talkasti remains clear.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Names are another good example. Indeed, look no further than our rulers. For most of our history, we were ruled by the Tullus dynasty, with Tullus very much being a name of Perican origin.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: However, we are now ruled by the Arkan dynasty, whose name can be traced back to early Talkasti nomads.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So where did the name Varlamore come from.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: The name comes from the River Varla that separated Talkasti and Perican territory. What was once a symbol of our divide, became the symbol of our unification.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was all super interesting! Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Not a problem, iknami!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going. Thanks again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about religion in Varlamore?

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Of course! Whereas in other parts of the world you'll likely find followers of many gods, here in Varlamore, you'll only really find those dedicated to Ralos and Ranul.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Losanium is the official term for this religion, though it is not a term you'll find used very often.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Losanium encompasses the worship of both Ralos and Ranul, and while they do indeed go hand in hand, it is rare to find individuals equally dedicated to both.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Instead, most people focus their worship on one of the two, with Ralos being by far the more popular. Something that isn't too surprising given Ranul's association with death.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Now that said, you might actually find a higher than normal number of Ranul worshippers right here in the museum.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Something that is easily forgotten is that while Ranul is indeed a god of death, he is also a god of learning and knowledge.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: If you wish to learn more, you may well want to seek out one of the teoki in the temple to the south. Though of course, they may well have somewhat biased takes on their religious practices.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll keep that in mind. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Don't mention it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about Varlamore's recent history?

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: I can indeed! When it comes to recent events in the kingdom, the best place to start is with the War of Betrayal. It all started around 100 years ago when Imafore Tullus ascended to the throne.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: He soon reorganised the kingdom into the Varlamorian Empire, a controversial choice to say the least, though it soon became clear that controversial choices were quite popular with Imafore.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: While public opinion was always mixed, nothing he did resulted in outright conflict until his sudden and brutal murder of the Kourend Royal Family. War was quickly declared.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Until that day, Varlamore had successfully managed to avoid conflict since before the kingdom was officially established. To say the war was unpopular would be an understatement.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Imafore soon found himself facing as much resistance from within as he did from Kourend. The end result was predictable.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Imafore and his forces found themselves trapped between two armies at the Battle of the Proudspire. He was captured and executed, bringing an end to the Tullus dynasty.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: This brings us to a fun little fact. After his death, Imafore was succeeded by King Mezpah Arkan. One of his first acts was to call for the beheading of every statue of Imafore in the kingdom.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: The statues remain to this day. It's actually a crime to try and remove them. They exist as a reminder of our history, and a warning to any who would try to follow in Imafore's footsteps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have to keep an eye out for them.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Indeed. I often like to challenge people to try and find them all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how has the kingdom changed since Imafore died?

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: The War of Betrayal was very much seen as a wake- up call. While Varlamore had once flourished under the traditions of the Tullus dynasty, by the end, it's fair to say we were stagnating.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: King Mezpah set to work on transforming the kingdom. His ambitions were high, to the point that much of what he desired never came to pass in his lifetime.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: One of his most notable goals was to see Varlamore open more widely to the outer world. A goal which thanks to his great-granddaughter, has now finally been achieved!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then I should be very thankful. Without him, I might never have been allowed to visit!

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: Indeed. We would often send our students on trips to the east prior to the opening of the borders. The amount of paperwork needed was astounding! Now we can just hop on a ship any time we like!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That definitely sounds much more fun. Thanks for all the information.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: It's not a problem, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. If I have any questions, I'll be sure to ask.
